mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:economic growth generally supports equity markets
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: If the annualized quarter-over-quarter growth rate of GDPC1 is positive, but the S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over the last 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months, but the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is very low and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows signs of accelerating, it indicates a potential for future monetary tightening to curb inflation, which can negatively impact equity valuations by increasing discount rates and reducing liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low rates with rising inflation often lead to tightening cycles
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is low and CPIAUCSL growth is high, but the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic weakness or recession, a significant decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals a severe contraction in consumer demand, which directly impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:retail sales are a key indicator of consumer health
failure_condition: If RSAFS declines by more than 5% annualized over 3 months during a declared recession, but the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in housing starts (HOUST) after a period of decline indicates renewed confidence in the economy and future consumer spending on housing-related goods, which typically precedes broader economic recovery and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average growth > 10% after a 6-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST shows a strong rebound after a decline, but the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent, strong growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, leading to increased consumer income and spending, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 150,000 jobs per month
evidence_refs: fact:job growth is crucial for economic expansion
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently grows by more than 150k jobs/month, but the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of very low Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, stimulates investment, and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for 6 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates generally support equity markets
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for 6 months, but the S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall industrial activity, indicating weakening economic fundamentals that negatively impact corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production is a key measure of economic health
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 3% annualized over 3 months, but the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.
